mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in real retail sales persists as a direct proxy for nominal consumer spending momentum, driving S&P 500 revenues and forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: RSAFS month-over-month change has positive sign but S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of observations over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate below 5.0% signals a tight labor market that constrains corporate margins through wage pressures, leading to negative earnings revisions and downward pressure on S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: When UNRATE < 5.0%, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in >60% of triggered instances, or the mean return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous occurrence of accelerating consumer prices and a suppressed federal funds rate below 1.0% generates deeply negative real yields that drive equity multiple expansion, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint z-score interaction term between CPIAUCSL monthly change and FEDFUNDS level fails to achieve |Rank IC| > 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth drives positive S&P 500 returns during early-cycle recoveries when output gaps are closing; monthly IP acceleration predicts 20-day positive returns specifically in this regime and not in late-cycle periods.
failure_condition: In periods classified as early-cycle recovery, INDPRO acceleration fails to correlate with positive 20-day SPX returns (walk-forward correlation < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: Monthly housing starts serve as a high-beta leading indicator of construction activity and durable goods demand; positive momentum in HOUST predicts positive S&P 500 returns over a one-week horizon as the market prices cyclical acceleration.
failure_condition: HOUST month-over-month changes and S&P 500 5-day forward returns exhibit negative correlation (r < -0.05) over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate below 0.5% sustains excess liquidity that compresses equity risk premiums and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as long as the nominal rate remains at the effective lower bound.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS < 0.5%, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in >50% of observations over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Coordinated growth in nonfarm payrolls and industrial production amplifies earnings visibility beyond independent single-series effects, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The interaction term between PAYEMS and INDPRO monthly changes produces a negative beta on S&P 500 60-day returns (p < 0.10) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month acceleration in industrial production above 0.5 percentage points predicts positive S&P 500 returns over a 5-day horizon as manufacturing surprises are rapidly priced into cyclical equities.
failure_condition: INDPRO accelerations above 0.5pp are followed by negative 5-day S&P 500 returns in >55% of instances over any 2-year rolling window.
